name: Observer
description: Define a one-to-many dependency between objects so that when one object changes state, all its dependents are notified and updated automatically.
problem: Need to notify multiple objects about changes without making them tightly coupled.
solution: Define Subject and Observer interfaces. Subject maintains list of observers and notifies them of changes.
when_to_use: When change to one object requires changing others, and you don't know how many
When an object should be able to notify others without knowing who they are
when_not_to_use: When observers need to know about each other
When notification chains could cause performance issues

participants.name: Subject
participants.role: Knows its observers, provides interface for attaching/detaching

participants.name: Observer
participants.role: Defines updating interface for objects that should be notified

participants.name: ConcreteSubject
participants.role: Stores state of interest, sends notification when state changes

participants.name: ConcreteObserver
participants.role: Maintains reference to ConcreteSubject, implements Observer update interface

pros: Loose coupling between subject and observers
cons: Memory leaks if observers not properly detached
